summary: FBI/CISA/MS-ISAC AA25-071A (March 12, 2025) StopRansomware advisory on Medusa RaaS (first identified June 2021; >300 victims as of Feb 2025 across healthcare, education, legal, insurance, tech, manufacturing). Evolved from closed to affiliate model; double/triple extortion (.medusa extension, AES-256 via gaze.exe, TOR chat/Tox negotiation, .onion leak site). Initial access via IABs (phishing T1566, CVEs like ScreenConnect CVE-2024-1709, Fortinet EMS CVE-2023-48788). LOTL discovery (Advanced IP Scanner, SoftPerfect, PowerShell/cmd/WMI). Evasion: base64/gzip PowerShell obfuscation (powerfun.ps1 stager), certutil download, EDR kill drivers. Lateral: 9+ RMM tools (AnyDesk/Atera/ConnectWise/etc) + PsExec/openrdp.bat + Mimikatz LSASS dump. Exfil: Rclone. Impact: gaze.exe terminates services/backups, deletes shadows, encrypts VMs. IOCs: 2 MD5 hashes (openrdp.bat, pu.exe), 5 emails (protonmail/cock.li), 50+ detection commands (Appendix A).

timeline.event: Medusa RaaS first identified (initially closed operations)
timeline.evidence_snippet: Medusa is a ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) variant first identified in June 2021

timeline.event: Medusa RaaS used in ransomware attacks from 2021 to present
timeline.evidence_snippet: The RaaS Medusa variant has been used to conduct ransomware attacks from 2021 to present

timeline.event: FBI investigations through February 2025; >300 victims identified across critical infrastructure sectors
timeline.evidence_snippet: As of February 2025, Medusa developers and affiliates have impacted over 300 victims
